# Taxonomy


## Crop Improvement and Genetics
_Covers the science and practice of developing improved crop varieties through genetic, genomic, and breeding approaches_

### Post-Harvest and Food Loss Management
_Post-harvest management, food loss reduction, and storage technologies_

    - **post-harvest losses** (freq: 5)
      _reduction in crop quantity or quality after harvest_
    - **post-harvest management** (freq: 1)
      _practices and technologies for handling, processing and storing crops after harvest_
    - **post-harvest processing** (freq: 3)
      _handling and treatment of crops after harvest_
    - **post-harvest technology** (freq: 2)
      _methods and equipment for processing, preserving, and storing agricultural products_
    - **post-harvest loss reduction** (freq: 1)
      _minimizing food waste and quality degradation after crop harvest through improved handling_
    - **postharvest loss reduction** (freq: 2)
      _strategies to minimize food waste after harvest_
    - **post-harvest food loss** (freq: 1)
      _reduction in food quantity or quality that occurs after harvest but before consumption_
    - **food loss and waste reduction** (freq: 1)
      _strategies to minimize food losses along value chains and consumer waste_
    - **hermetic storage** (freq: 1)
      _airtight storage systems that prevent pest infestation and maintain grain quality_
    - **on-farm seed storage** (freq: 1)
      _farmer-managed preservation of seeds between harvest and planting_
    - **cold chain transportation** (freq: 1)
      _temperature-controlled logistics system for preserving product quality during transport_
    - **two-step drying** (freq: 1)
      _seed processing technique using initial warm drying followed by cooler drying to improve storage longevity_
    - **parboiling** (freq: 1)
      _rice processing technique involving partial boiling in the husk to improve nutritional quality_
    - **gem parboiling technique** (freq: 2)
      _improved rice processing method for enhanced quality and efficiency_
    - **food recovery** (freq: 2)
      _systematic collection and redistribution of surplus food to reduce waste and improve access_
    - **food rescue** (freq: 2)
      _recovery and redistribution of surplus food to reduce waste and improve food security_
    - **waste-to-value technologies** (freq: 1)
      _methods to convert organic waste into useful products like compost, biogas, or protein_
    - **resource recovery and reuse** (freq: 1)
      _circular economy approach to extract value from waste materials including biomass and wastewater_
    - **traceability** (freq: 2)
      _ability to track products through supply chain stages_
    - **traceability systems** (freq: 1)
      _systems for tracking products through supply chains_
    - **quality assurance** (freq: 2)
      _systematic processes to ensure products or services meet specified standards_
    - **quality control** (freq: 2)
      _systematic processes to ensure product standards_
    - **quality management systems** (freq: 1)
      _structured framework for ensuring consistent product quality and safety standards_
    - **food safety** (freq: 6)
      _assurance that food will not cause harm when prepared or consumed_
    - **food safety management** (freq: 2)
      _systematic approach to ensuring safe food production and handling_
    - **food safety monitoring** (freq: 2)
      _systematic surveillance of contaminants and hazards in food systems_
    - **food safety training** (freq: 1)
      _education programs to improve food handling and safety practices_
    - **aflatoxin contamination** (freq: 1)
      _presence of toxic compounds produced by fungi that cause liver cancer and stunting_
    - **pesticide residue detection** (freq: 1)
      _analytical techniques to identify and quantify chemical residues in crops_
